evidence: Higgins, Song, Warr and Sherwood Lollar 2025 modeled Revell Batholith radiolytic H2 and sulfate using Monte Carlo; most probable H2 rate 1.6 nmol m-3 rock yr-1 and sulfate 10^2-10^6 lower than Kidd Creek
interpretation: Revell is a benchmark for low-porosity crystalline rock habitability and DGR gas/source-term risk, not an economic H2 resource benchmark
provenance: JGR Biogeosciences DOI 10.1029/2025JG008863; abstract indexed via AGU/EBSCO/IvySci
data_gap: Full input distributions for U, Th, K, porosity and sulfide were not extracted in this run
next_step: Retrieve article supplementary data and reproduce Monte Carlo

evidence: Revell Batholith is selected for Canada's DGR; repository depth discussed as 650-800 m crystalline rock; groundwater expected anoxic saline Ca-Na-Cl ~0.1 mol/L Cl-
interpretation: Deep, saline, anoxic, low-permeability crystalline environment is ideal for testing long-term radiolysis, diffusion, sulfate and gas-pressure models
provenance: NWMO site page; Briggs et al. 2025; Revell geophysical/geology papers
data_gap: Exact borehole U-Th-K and fracture-water chemistry dataset not parsed

evidence: Water radiolysis yields e_aq-, H radicals, OH radicals, H3O+, H2 and H2O2; H2 yield depends on LET and oxide/water interfaces
interpretation: Natural alpha radiation near mineral-water interfaces is disproportionately important for H2 per deposited energy
provenance: Le Caer 2011 Water review; Dzaugis et al. 2016 Frontiers
data_gap: Mineral-specific G-values for Revell/Athabasca rocks unknown
next_step: Measure G(H2), G(H2O2) on actual core powders and fracture chips

evidence: Dzaugis et al. showed fracture width strongly changes H2 production normalized to water volume; 1 micrometer fractures can be far more productive per water volume than 10 cm fractures
interpretation: The controlling variable is not bulk U alone, but U-Th-K energy deposition intersecting small water volumes and high mineral-water surface area
confidence: High for principle; Medium for target areas
provenance: Dzaugis et al. 2016; Revell low-porosity Monte Carlo result
data_gap: Revell/Athabasca connected fracture aperture distribution not compiled
next_step: Use micro-CT, fracture logs and hydraulic tests to estimate water-energy interception factor

evidence: Kidd Creek/Canadian Shield studies show radiolysis oxidants can oxidize sulfide minerals to sulfate; sulfate supports H2-consuming sulfate reduction
interpretation: Sulfides convert radiolysis from one-product H2 source into a coupled redox battery; this helps biosphere but may suppress H2 accumulation
provenance: Li et al. 2016 Nature Communications; Higgins et al. 2025
data_gap: Revell sulfide abundance is low; Athabasca sulfide/graphite shear distribution heterogeneous
next_step: Map pyrite/pyrrhotite abundance, sulfur isotopes and sulfate concentration in fracture waters

evidence: Athabasca Basin contains U-rich brines; fluid inclusions in barren basin rocks contain 0.6-26.8 ppm U, while ore fluids can reach much higher values; 2025 GSC/Mendeley data show eastern Athabasca felsic intrusives mostly 1.6-3.5 µW m-3 heat production with pegmatitic elevations
interpretation: Athabasca basement may host local radiolytic H2 hotspots around radiogenic granitoids, pegmatites and U mineralization, but preservation depends on fractures and sinks
provenance: Chi et al. 2019 Scientific Reports; Powell et al. 2025 Mendeley Data
data_gap: No direct H2 or fracture-water dataset for Athabasca basement compiled
next_step: Screen Athabasca drillholes for U-Th-K, gamma logs, porosity, fracture water and dissolved gas

evidence: DGR radiolysis models consider H2/O2/H2O2 partitioning, chloride radiolysis, copper corrosion and bentonite interactions; long-term dose and near-field chemistry matter
interpretation: Natural U-Th-K radiolysis is a background source term; used-fuel gamma field is a separate near-field transient source that can dominate early repository chemistry
provenance: Briggs et al. 2025 MDPI; NWMO documentation
data_gap: Need coupled natural-background plus repository-source model

evidence: Revell rate converts to only ~1.6 mol H2 per km3 rock per year; even over geologic time accumulation requires exceptional sealing and low consumption
interpretation: Low-porosity shield rocks are more important for deep biosphere and safety than near-term commercial H2 production
confidence: High for Revell; Medium for broader crystalline provinces
provenance: Higgins et al. 2025; calculations in this report
data_gap: No trap-scale natural gas inventory or flux data
next_step: Use H2 accumulation only where source, migration, trap and low biological sink coincide

evidence: Radiolytic Hydrogen System Index combines radiogenic source, interface geometry, water residence, sink balance and trap retention rather than U-Th-K alone
interpretation: This helps compare Revell, Kidd Creek, Athabasca basement and DGR settings in a single geochemical framework
data_gap: Needs calibration against measured dissolved H2/sulfate/noble gas datasets
next_step: Build Bayesian model using U-Th-K, porosity, sulfide, noble gases and H2 data
